pairs.question: Who was the first Baron Denman and what title was he granted?
pairs.answer: The first Baron Denman was Thomas Denman, a prominent lawyer, judge, and Whig politician. He was granted the title 'Baron Denman, of Dovedale in the County of Derby' in the Peerage of the United Kingdom in 1834.

pairs.question: What role did the second Baron Denman hold in the family lineage and what surname did he adopt?
pairs.answer: The second Baron Denman was the son of the first Baron. In 1876, he adopted the additional surname 'Aitchison' by royal licence, which was the surname of his father-in-law.

pairs.question: Which Baron Denman served as Governor-General of Australia, and during what years?
pairs.answer: Lord Denman, the third Baron Denman, served as Governor-General of Australia from 1911 to 1914.

pairs.question: How did the title of Baron Denman pass from the fourth to the fifth Baron?
pairs.answer: The fourth Baron Denman died in 1971, and the title passed to his first cousin, Sir Charles Denman, who became the fifth Baron Denman.

pairs.question: What is the Denman baronetcy and when was it created?
pairs.answer: The Denman baronetcy, of Staffield in the County of Cumberland, is a title in the Baronetage of the United Kingdom. It was created in 1945 for the Hon. Richard Denman, younger brother of the third Baron Denman.

pairs.question: Describe the political career of the first Baronet of the Denman baronetcy.
pairs.answer: The first Baronet of the Denman baronetcy, Hon. Richard Denman, represented Carlisle in the House of Commons as a Liberal from January 1910 until 1918. He later represented Leeds Central as a Labour MP from 1929 to 1931, and as a National Labour MP from 1931 to 1945.

pairs.question: How did the title of Baron Denman and the Denman baronetcy eventually become linked?
pairs.answer: The second Baronet of the Denman baronetcy, who became the fifth Baron Denman in 1971, was the son of the first Baronet. This connection linked the Denman baronetcy to the Baron Denman title.
